name: Broadcom Clarity (formerly CA Clarity / Niku) PPM API
what_it_does: Broadcom Clarity (formerly CA Clarity PPM / Niku) project portfolio management REST API for enterprise IT PMO, investment planning, resource capacity management, and project financial tracking. Enables AI agents to manage project and program creation for portfolio intake automation, handle resource capacity and demand management for workforce planning automation, access investment portfolio prioritization and scoring for strategic alignment automation, retrieve project financial tracking and budget management for IT spend automation, manage risk and issue tracking for project governance automation, handle time tracking and labor cost allocation for IT project accounting, access portfolio analytics and executive dashboard data for CIO business intelligence reporting, retrieve skills and competency management for resource matching automation, manage project milestone and deliverable tracking for automated status reporting, and integrate Clarity with SAP, Oracle, ServiceNow, and Agile platforms (Jira, Azure DevOps) for enterprise PMO workflow.
use_cases: Managing project portfolios from IT PMO agents
Planning resource capacity from workforce planning agents
Tracking IT investments from portfolio governance agents
Reporting project status from executive dashboard agents
not_for: Construction and capital project controls (use InEight or Oracle P6)
Agile-only development teams without portfolio governance
Small PMOs without enterprise license budget
best_when: A large enterprise IT PMO or transformation office wanting AI agents to automate project portfolio management, resource capacity planning, IT investment prioritization, and project financial tracking within Broadcom Clarity.
avoid_when: BROADCOM ACQUISITION PRODUCT CONSOLIDATION RISK: Broadcom acquired CA Technologies (formerly Computer Associates) in 2018; Clarity PPM is subject to Broadcom's portfolio rationalization strategy; verify current product investment, roadmap continuity, and customer support commitment before new Clarity integration investment. RESOURCE CAPACITY ACCURACY FOR REGULATED PROJECTS: Automated resource capacity and allocation data from Clarity used in government contract labor reporting must reflect actual hours worked, not planned allocation; automated DCAA time reporting using Clarity planned data without actual timesheet validation creates labor mischarging risk. SOX IT GENERAL CONTROLS FOR PPM DATA CHANGES: Automated modification of project budget baselines, resource allocations, or investment priorities in Clarity requires change control documentation for SOX IT general controls; automated Clarity changes without audit trail create SOX ITGC finding. LEGACY SOAP API DEPENDENCY: Clarity has legacy SOAP/XML web services alongside REST API; older enterprise integrations may use deprecated SOAP endpoints; validate current API type before building new automated workflows.

auth.notes: Broadcom Clarity uses OAuth 2.0 and API key authentication. REST API with JSON and legacy SOAP/XML. San Jose, California HQ (Broadcom Inc., NASDAQ: AVGO). Broadcom acquired CA Technologies 2018 for $18.9B. Originally Niku software, acquired by CA Technologies 2005. Enterprise IT PPM for Fortune 500 and large government. Competes with Planview, Oracle Primavera Cloud, and Microsoft Project for enterprise PPM.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — enterprise annual subscription with user-based licensing
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual enterprise subscription by named or concurrent user count; SaaS and on-premise options
pricing.notes: San Jose CA. Broadcom NASDAQ:AVGO. CA Technologies acquisition 2018 ($18.9B). Originally Niku (2005). Fortune 500 enterprise IT PMO target. Per-user annual subscription.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; complex portfolio and capacity queries throttled; batch export for large datasets

known_agent_gotchas: BROADCOM ACQUISITION API STABILITY RISK: Broadcom's acquisition of CA Technologies and history of product portfolio rationalization creates API investment uncertainty; Broadcom has discontinued several CA products post-acquisition; verify current Clarity API support commitment and roadmap before investing in new Clarity integration

LEGACY SOAP API ALONGSIDE REST: Clarity has both legacy SOAP/XML web services and newer REST API; many enterprise integrations built on SOAP endpoints; verify which API type a specific data domain uses before building automation; REST API coverage is incomplete for some legacy Clarity modules

NO WEBHOOKS — PROJECT EVENTS REQUIRE POLLING: Clarity does not support native webhooks for project status changes, resource allocation events, or budget updates; automated workflow must implement polling-based event detection with appropriate interval

Resource capacity vs actual timesheet discrepancy — Clarity resource demand planning uses allocation data; actual project time from timesheet entries often diverges from planned allocation; automated reporting using Clarity capacity data without timesheet reconciliation produces inaccurate labor cost and utilization metrics

On-premise deployment complexity for cloud agents — large Clarity deployments are still on-premise; cloud AI agents require VPN or reverse proxy to access on-premise Clarity REST API; SaaS Clarity deployment required for direct cloud agent integration without network complexity

SOX ITGC change control for portfolio data — automated modification of Clarity investment baselines, resource plans, or project financial data in SOX-scope environments requires change management audit trail; automated Clarity updates without system-generated change log create SOX IT general controls audit finding
agent_readiness.security_notes: Enterprise PPM. SOC2, ISO27001. OAuth2/API key. US/EU. IT project portfolio and resource capacity data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Enterprise PPM. SOC2, ISO27001. OAuth2/API key. US/EU. IT portfolio and project financial data.
